reason: UNPROVENANCED LOAD-BEARING FACT (round-1 ch-a30c07d51e448f88 not genuinely resolved). The node derives R^2=(S^2)^(-1) solely from 'By GT-funcalc the function calculus X|->f(X) is multiplicative: f(S^2)g(S^2)=(fg)(S^2)'. GT-funcalc's external description asserts 'the calculus is multiplicative f(X)g(X)=(fg)(X)', but its declared locus refs/kitaev-2405.02434/approximate_algebras.tex:503-532 does NOT contain this. Byte-match of :503-510 (eqs Taylor_simple / Taylor_simple_bound, the Banach-algebra Taylor calculus actually used here): f(X) is stated only to be 'well-defined, commutes with X', and to satisfy the norm bound ||f(X)-f(x0 I)|| <= sum |a_n| ||X-x0 I||^n. No multiplicativity/homomorphism statement appears anywhere in 503-532 (verified by grep over the full file: the function-calculus homomorphism property appears ONLY at line 552, which is the HOLOMORPHIC calculus -- outside the cited 503-532 locus AND explicitly disclaimed by Kitaev at line 535: 'We have not found use of these methods due to their fragility in the approximate setting'). Thus the multiplicativity claim is an uncited fact merely relocated into the external's prose; it is not backed by a byte-match at the cited source. Per the project rule 'NO standard facts -- every fact must be provenanced against ground truth in refs/', this fails. It is the SINGLE load-bearing fact: R^2=(S^2)^(-1) => sgn(S)^2=S^2 R^2=I => P^2=P. Remedy: either provenance multiplicativity of the Taylor/holomorphic calculus to a valid refs/ source (note the only in-refs route, holomorphic calc at :552, is disclaimed by Kitaev for the approximate setting and would itself need that spec(S^2) avoids (-inf,0]), OR derive R^2=(S^2)^(-1) explicitly via Cauchy-product + binomial-convolution sum_{k+j=m}C(-1/2,k)C(-1/2,j)=(-1)^m + Neumann-series sum(-1)^m U^m=(I+U)^(-1) (which then needs GT-binomial-series, itself flagged 'PROVENANCE GAP: no standalone analysis source in refs/').
